title: Understanding Angular Momentum
text: Angular momentum (L) is given by the formula $$L=r p sin\left(\theta\right)$$, where $$r$$ is the radius vector, $$p$$ is the linear momentum, and theta is the angle between $$r$$ and $$p$$.

title: Calculate Linear Momentum
text: The linear momentum (p) of the bird is given by $$p=m v$$, where $$m$$ is the mass and v is the velocity of the bird.

title: Calculate Linear Momentum
text: Given the mass of the bird is $$2.0$$ kg and its velocity is $$20.0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, calculate the linear momentum.

title: Determine the Radius Vector
text: The radius vector (r) is the distance from the point where you are standing to the bird. Given the altitude of the bird is $$300.0$$ $$m$$, use trigonometry to find $$r$$.

title: Calculate Radius Vector
text: Assuming the bird is directly overhead, the radius vector is equal to the altitude. Calculate $$r$$.

title: Calculate Angular Momentum
text: Use the formula $$L=r p sin\left(\theta\right)$$ to calculate the angular momentum. Assume theta is $$90$$ degrees, so $$sin(theta)=1$$.

title: Final Calculation
text: Substitute the values $$r=300$$, $$p=40$$, and $$sin(theta)=1$$ into the formula $$L=r p sin\left(\theta\right)$$.
